title: Iran warns of decisive force against ground invasion | 7NEWS
description: Tehran has warned that any ground invasion will be met with decisive force following reports of a US-backed plan to arm Kurdish forces to incite an uprising within Iran. Air raid sirens have sounded in Tel Aviv as cluster warheads rained down over central Israel, causing fires and damage. Iran's Foreign Minister has stated the country is prepared for a ground offensive, even if US troops are deployed, while Israel has celebrated its first aerial dogfight in 40 years with an F-35 shooting down an Iranian trainer jet. title: Trump seeks role in choosing Iran's next leader | 7NEWS
description: Donald Trump has stated he wants direct involvement in selecting Iran's next supreme leader following the death of Ali Khamenei in recent strikes. The US president rejected Khamenei's son as a candidate, calling him a lightweight, and vowed to ensure Iran's next leader will not threaten America or its neighbours. Trump compared his approach to his intervention in Venezuela, where he ordered Nicolas Maduro's arrest and replacement in January. title: Stranded Australians arrive home from Dubai war zone | 7NEWS
description: A repatriation flight from Dubai carrying more than 290 passengers has landed at Melbourne's Tullamarine Airport, bringing stranded Australians home from the Middle East war zone. Many travellers had their previous flights cancelled due to widespread airspace closures caused by military conflict in the UAE and surrounding regions. More than 100,000 Australians remain stranded in the Middle East as the federal government and DFAT coordinate a massive repatriation effort amid ongoing travel disruptions. title: Iranian women's team sings anthem amid protests | 7NEWS
description: The Iranian women's football team sang their national anthem before their Asian Cup match against the Matildas at Cbus Stadium on the Gold Coast, reversing their silent stance from their opening game. Around 100 protesters demonstrated outside the stadium, with demonstrators claiming the players are hostages to the Iranian regime. The Australian government faces pressure to provide asylum for the team, with warnings from independent Iranian journalist and political analyst Ali Bornaei that the players face arbitrary detention and execution if they return to Iran. title: Australia bans Hizb ut-Tahrir under hate laws | 7NEWS
description: Hizb ut-Tahrir has been banned under Australia's new hate group laws, making it a criminal offence to be a member or provide training or funds to the organisation. title: Britney Spears arrested for driving under the influence | 7NEWS
description: Britney Spears was arrested and charged with driving under the influence following an alleged hour-long car chase in California. title: The discount trap costing Australians millions | 7NEWS
description: Approximately 41 per cent of Australians are falling victim to retail marketing tactics that encourage impulse purchases through free shipping thresholds and bundled offers. Money expert Rebecca Pike from Finder explains how these seemingly attractive discounts trick consumers into spending more money on items they don't need. The psychological trap works by making shoppers believe they're saving money when they're actually exceeding their intended budget to qualify for deals. title: Coroner rules dingo attack caused backpacker's death | 7NEWS
description: A Queensland coroner has ruled that 19-year-old Canadian backpacker Piper James died from drowning as a consequence of a dingo attack on K'gari on 19 January. Eight dingoes identified through ear tags have been put down by the Department of Environment, with operations ongoing. title: Raising boys in the age of online masculinity influencers | 7NEWS
description: Dr Zac Seidler, a men's mental health expert, addresses the impact of online influencers on young men's understanding of masculinity and mental wellbeing. He discusses the loneliness and suicide epidemics affecting men, and how boys are finding community in harmful online spaces rather than face-to-face connections. Seidler emphasises the importance of diverse male role models and parental curiosity in raising resilient young men, advocating for positive aspirational models rather than focusing solely on what not to do. title: Former US Marine breaks his arm during protest in Congress
description: The arm of a protester was broken as security officers tried to remove him for interrupting a meeting at the US Capitol on March 4. The protestor has been identified as US Marine veteran Brian McGinnis. While authorities attempted to pull McGinnis out of the room, his left hand became stuck between the door and the frame.  McGinnis has been charged with resisting arrest and three counts of assaulting a police officer. #protest #marine #Congress #USpolitics
